title: vicia canescens subsp. leucomala
intro: Vicia canescens subsp. leucomala, also known as the pale tufted vetch, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the legume family. This plant is used in traditional medicine and is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Vicia canescens subsp. leucomala include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and act as a diuretic. Vicia canescens subsp. leucomala can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used in folk medicine to treat various ailments.
